vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1496: bug(flywheel-settings): 'extra-high' effort option missing from Flywheel role config UI (only low/medium/high shown)

plan.title: bug(flywheel-settings): 'extra-high' effort option missing from Flywheel role config UI (only low/medium/high shown)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: Operators cannot select the 'extra-high' or 'max' thinking-budget tier for the Flywheel orchestrator from Settings → Roles → Flywheel. The role config dropdown renders only low/medium/high because both FlywheelEffort (Schema.Literals in @overdeck/contracts) and RoleEffort (src/lib/config-yaml.ts) cap at 'high'. Meanwhile the conversation composer EffortPicker (src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/chat/EffortPicker.tsx) already exposes five tiers — low, medium, high, xhigh, max — so the Flywheel role config is the lone holdout. The chosen Flywheel effort propagates through Cloister → spawnRun → getRoleRuntimeBaseCommand which passes `--effort <level>` directly to the claude CLI, so widening the literal at the contract and config layers is enough to unlock the new tiers end-to-end.
narratives.Proposal: Widen the effort literal across the contract package and the canonical RoleEffort type to ['low', 'medium', 'high', 'xhigh', 'max'], matching the chat EffortPicker IDs exactly. Update every validator (settings-api, config-yaml) and inline duplicate type declaration so typecheck passes. Add the two new <option> tags to the Flywheel effort <select> in RolesPanel. Keep the default Flywheel effort at 'high' so existing operators see no behavior change. Do NOT add model-aware availableLevels gating in RolesPanel (out of scope; chat composer has that, RolesPanel does not). Do NOT add a `pan flywheel start --effort` flag — Flywheel reads effort from settings; only `pan plan --effort` and `pan strike --effort` (which already exist) get their help strings widened. The work is decomposed into 9 small beads with B1 (the @overdeck/contracts FlywheelEffort widening) as the inspected foundation.

autoDecisions.summary: Add both 'xhigh' AND 'max' (five tiers total), not just 'extra-high'.
autoDecisions.rationale: Existing chat composer EFFORT_LEVELS in EffortPicker.tsx already enumerates ['low','medium','high','xhigh','max']. Treating Flywheel role config as a different vocabulary would create two parallel effort dictionaries for operators to memorize. Mirroring the chat picker is the simplest defensible choice and removes drift between two UIs that already exist.

EffortPicker uses 'xhigh' as the stored ID with label 'Extra High'; localStorage entries under EFFORT_STORAGE_KEY already store this value. Switching to a hyphenated 'extra-high' string would fork from established precedent and from whatever Claude Code's CLI flag accepts.

autoDecisions.summary: Keep default Flywheel effort at 'high' rather than promoting to 'xhigh'.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue text explicitly says 'Not urgent — operator can run the flywheel at high for now'. Silently moving the default to a higher thinking budget would change runtime cost without operator consent.

autoDecisions.summary: No model-aware availableLevels gating in RolesPanel.
autoDecisions.rationale: RolesPanel currently renders the three effort options unconditionally regardless of which Flywheel model is selected; chat composer's per-model gating is not mirrored here today. Adding gating now is scope creep relative to a 'dropdown is missing two options' bug. If a model doesn't accept the chosen tier, Claude Code silently caps — pre-existing behavior. Hazard H4 documents this for follow-up.

autoDecisions.summary: No new `pan flywheel start --effort` CLI flag.
autoDecisions.rationale: `pan flywheel start` reads effort from role config (config.yaml / settings.json), not from CLI flags. The issue's fix sketch mentioning `pan flywheel start --effort extra-high` describes a flag that doesn't exist today. Adding it is a separate feature. The CLI surface change is limited to widening the help text and validation of the existing `pan plan --effort` and `pan strike --effort` options.

autoDecisions.summary: Replace inline `'low' | 'medium' | 'high'` types with `RoleEffort` import where they appear.
autoDecisions.rationale: Several call sites (src/lib/agents.ts, src/lib/planning/spawn-planning-session.ts, src/cli/commands/plan.ts, src/cli/commands/strike.ts, src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/PlanDialog.tsx, etc.) currently inline the literal. Widening each inline copy is a chore that invites future drift; importing the canonical type is the same diff size and removes the drift risk for the next time someone adds an effort tier.

items.title: Widen FlywheelEffort schema literal in @overdeck/contracts to include 'xhigh' and 'max'

narrative.Action: In packages/contracts/src/flywheel.ts: (1) widen `FlywheelEffort = Schema.Literals(["low", "medium", "high"])` to `Schema.Literals(["low", "medium", "high", "xhigh", "max"])`; (2) widen the matching interface field `effort: "low" | "medium" | "high"` on FlywheelOrchestrator to `"low" | "medium" | "high" | "xhigh" | "max"`; (3) export a named type alias `FlywheelEffortValue = typeof FlywheelEffort.Type` to give downstream packages a single import surface. Then rebuild the contracts package (`cd packages/contracts && npm run build`) so the new dist is consumable by the dashboard server and the frontend. This bead is the foundation — every downstream bead consumes the widened type.

subItems.title: FlywheelEffort.Schema decodes 'xhigh' and 'max' without error and continues to decode 'low', 'medium', 'high'

FlywheelOrchestrator interface's `effort` field type union includes all five literals; typecheck passes against the new union in both the contracts package and any consumer that imports FlywheelOrchestrator
`cd packages/contracts && npm run build` succeeds and the new dist exposes the widened literal (verifiable by importing FlywheelEffort from @overdeck/contracts in a sibling file)

items.title: Widen RoleEffort type and config.yaml validator in src/lib/config-yaml.ts

narrative.Action: In src/lib/config-yaml.ts: (1) widen `export type RoleEffort = 'low' | 'medium' | 'high'` (line 296) to include 'xhigh' and 'max'; (2) update the validator branch around lines 1474-1475 so the allow-list and the thrown error message both reference the five tiers. Keep the default value at line 366 (`effort: 'high'`) unchanged.

subItems.title: `RoleEffort` type union is `'low' | 'medium' | 'high' | 'xhigh' | 'max'`
config.yaml validator accepts `roles.flywheel.effort: xhigh` and `roles.flywheel.effort: max` without throwing
Validator error message text is updated so it no longer claims 'must be low, medium, or high' — the new text enumerates all five tiers

items.title: Widen effort validation and matching test in src/lib/settings-api.ts and its test file

narrative.Action: In src/lib/settings-api.ts around lines 382-384: replace the hard-coded `effort !== 'low' && effort !== 'medium' && effort !== 'high'` check with a check against the widened RoleEffort literal (or a reusable allow-list constant). Update the pushed error message accordingly. In src/lib/__tests__/settings-api.test.ts line 614, update the expected error string AND add a positive-path assertion that `xhigh` and `max` are both accepted by validateSettings without errors.

subItems.title: validateSettings accepts roles.flywheel.effort = 'xhigh' and 'max' with zero errors
validateSettings still rejects an unrecognized value (e.g. 'extreme') with an error message that lists all five accepted tiers
settings-api.test.ts existing failing-path assertion is updated to match the new error string; the test file passes under `npm test -- settings-api`

items.title: Widen effort types in src/lib/agents.ts and src/lib/planning/spawn-planning-session.ts

narrative.Action: Replace inline `effort?: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high'` declarations with `effort?: RoleEffort` imports: (1) src/lib/agents.ts line 358 (getRoleRuntimeBaseCommand parameter) and line 1990 (SpawnRunOptions field); (2) src/lib/planning/spawn-planning-session.ts line 121 and line 165. The actual runtime behavior — passing `--effort ${effort}` to the claude CLI — is unchanged; this bead is type-level alignment so typecheck passes after B1 and B2 widen the literal.

subItems.title: `getRoleRuntimeBaseCommand` accepts `effort = 'xhigh'` and `effort = 'max'` at the type level and emits `--effort xhigh` / `--effort max` in the returned shell command (verifiable by unit-testing the returned string)
SpawnRunOptions.effort field type matches RoleEffort; agents.ts compiles under `npm run typecheck` with no `Type '"xhigh"' is not assignable` errors
spawn-planning-session.ts buildPlanningPrompt and exported types accept all five effort tiers; typecheck passes

items.title: Widen effort types in CLI plan + strike commands and update --effort option help strings

narrative.Action: In src/cli/index.ts: (1) update the `--effort` option description on line 369 (`Planning effort: low | medium | high`) and line 520 (`Strike effort: low | medium | high (default medium)`) to enumerate all five tiers — `low | medium | high | xhigh | max`; (2) widen the inline action signature at line 522. In src/cli/commands/plan.ts line 9 and src/cli/commands/strike.ts line 16, replace inline `'low' | 'medium' | 'high'` with `RoleEffort` import from src/lib/config-yaml. Do NOT add a `pan flywheel start --effort` flag — that does not exist today and is out of scope per D4.

subItems.title: `pan plan <id> --effort xhigh` and `pan plan <id> --effort max` parse without commander rejecting the value, and the parsed effort reaches buildPlanningPrompt unchanged
`pan strike <ids…> --effort xhigh` and `--effort max` parse and reach the spawnRun call site at the action handler
`pan plan --help` and `pan strike --help` both show the widened option description `low | medium | high | xhigh | max`

items.title: Add 'Extra High' and 'Max' options to the Flywheel effort dropdown in RolesPanel

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Settings/RolesPanel.tsx: (1) widen `type Effort = 'low' | 'medium' | 'high'` at line 10 to include 'xhigh' and 'max' (or import the widened type from contracts/config-yaml — pick whichever matches the existing import pattern in this file); (2) inside the Flywheel effort <select> block at lines 526-536, add two new <option> tags after the existing 'high' option: `<option value="xhigh">Extra High</option>` and `<option value="max">Max</option>`. Match the casing/labels used by EffortPicker.tsx so the operator sees the same choices in both places. This is the user-visible bug fix anchor.

subItems.title: Settings → Roles → Flywheel role card renders a 'Flywheel effort' select with exactly five options in this order: Low, Medium, High, Extra High, Max

Selecting 'Extra High' triggers a PUT /api/settings with `roles.flywheel.effort: 'xhigh'`; selecting 'Max' sends `roles.flywheel.effort: 'max'`. Both succeed (200) and the value persists across page reload
Existing RolesPanel.test.tsx default-value assertion (`Flywheel effort` toHaveValue 'high') continues to pass — the saved default is unchanged

When the Flywheel orchestrator is then spawned via `pan flywheel start`, the resulting claude command line contains `--effort xhigh` (or `--effort max`) — verifiable by inspecting the launched tmux session's command or the spawnRun call args

items.title: Widen inline effort types in PlanDialog and FlywheelConversationPane frontend components

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/PlanDialog.tsx line 103 (`useState<'low' | 'medium' | 'high'>('medium')`), widen the generic to include 'xhigh' and 'max'. In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/flywheel/FlywheelConversationPane.tsx line 40 (`effort?: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high'`), widen the optional prop. Default values remain unchanged. PlanDialog's <select> can optionally surface the new options if it already mirrors EffortPicker, but adding UI options there is a separate concern — at minimum, type-widen so the state can hold the new values when imported from a parent. Confirm by inspection whether the picker UI already gates choices; if it does, no UI change needed; if it lists three hard-coded options, leave them as-is (out of scope for this bug).

subItems.title: PlanDialog effort state type accepts 'xhigh' and 'max' at the type level; the component compiles under `npm run typecheck`
FlywheelConversationPane's `effort?` prop type accepts all five tiers; consumer call sites compile

items.title: Update effort column comment in src/lib/database/schema.ts so it reflects the widened tier list

narrative.Action: In src/lib/database/schema.ts around line 424, update the comment `-- effort level (e.g. 'low', 'medium', 'high')` to include the two new tiers so future readers don't assume the column is restricted to the old three values. This is doc-only; the column type is TEXT and no migration is required.

subItems.title: Comment text on the effort column in schema.ts mentions all five tiers ('low', 'medium', 'high', 'xhigh', 'max')

items.title: Add test coverage for the widened effort tier across contract decode, settings API, and RolesPanel UI

narrative.Action: Add three layers of regression coverage so a future revert of any one bead fails CI: (1) Contract decode test — packages/contracts/src/__tests__ (or wherever flywheel contract tests live) verifies `Schema.decodeUnknownSync(FlywheelEffort)` accepts all five values and rejects an unknown one like 'extreme'. (2) Settings API positive-path test — extend src/lib/__tests__/settings-api.test.ts to assert validateSettings accepts xhigh and max with zero errors. (3) UI test — extend src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Settings/__tests__/RolesPanel.test.tsx to assert the Flywheel effort select renders five <option> elements with values low/medium/high/xhigh/max and labels matching EffortPicker. Do not assert the default — the existing test already covers that.

subItems.title: A contracts-layer test exists that decodes 'xhigh' and 'max' through FlywheelEffort and asserts they round-trip; same test asserts 'extreme' is rejected
settings-api.test.ts asserts validateSettings accepts roles.flywheel.effort = 'xhigh' and 'max' with empty errors array
RolesPanel.test.tsx asserts the Flywheel effort select renders five <option> tags whose values are exactly ['low','medium','high','xhigh','max'] in that order
Full suite `npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm test` passes from the workspace root
